concepts.name: Vector
concepts.short_definition: A quantity having direction as well as magnitude, often represented as an array of numbers.

concepts.name: Scalar
concepts.short_definition: A quantity that has magnitude but no direction, often represented by a single number.

concepts.name: Real Numbers
concepts.short_definition: Numbers that can be represented by a point on a number line.

concepts.name: Complex Numbers
concepts.short_definition: Numbers of the form a + bi, where a and b are real numbers and i is the imaginary unit.

concepts.name: Vector Space
concepts.short_definition: A set of vectors that can be added together and multiplied by scalars, satisfying certain axioms.

concepts.name: Field
concepts.short_definition: A set with two operations (addition and multiplication) that satisfy certain axioms, like real or complex numbers.

concepts.name: Inner Product
concepts.short_definition: A function that takes two vectors and returns a scalar, generalizing the dot product.

concepts.name: Euclidean Inner Product
concepts.short_definition: The standard inner product for vectors in Euclidean space.

concepts.name: Inner Product Axioms
concepts.short_definition: The defining properties of an inner product: linearity, symmetry/conjugate symmetry, and positive-definiteness.

concepts.name: Linearity
concepts.short_definition: The property that an operation distributes over addition and scalar multiplication.

concepts.name: Conjugate Symmetry
concepts.short_definition: The property that the inner product of two vectors is the complex conjugate of the inner product of the vectors in reverse order.

concepts.name: Positive-Definiteness
concepts.short_definition: The property that the inner product of a vector with itself is non-negative and zero only if the vector is the zero vector.

concepts.name: Orthogonality
concepts.short_definition: Two vectors are orthogonal if their inner product is zero.

concepts.name: Orthonormal Vectors
concepts.short_definition: A set of orthogonal vectors where each vector has a magnitude of one.

concepts.name: Vector Magnitude
concepts.short_definition: The length of a vector, often derived from the inner product.

concepts.name: Cauchy-Schwarz Inequality
concepts.short_definition: An inequality relating the inner product of two vectors to their magnitudes.

concepts.name: Triangle Inequality
concepts.short_definition: The sum of the lengths of any two sides of a triangle is greater than or equal to the length of the third side.

concepts.name: Metric Space
concepts.short_definition: A set where a distance function (metric) is defined, often derived from a norm.

concepts.name: Inner Product Space
concepts.short_definition: A vector space equipped with an inner product.

concepts.name: Hilbert Space
concepts.short_definition: A complete inner product space.

concepts.name: Completeness
concepts.short_definition: A property of a metric space where every Cauchy sequence converges to a point within the space.

concepts.name: Cauchy Sequence
concepts.short_definition: A sequence of points in a metric space such that the distance between any two terms becomes arbitrarily small as the terms get further into the sequence.

concepts.name: Orthogonal Complement
concepts.short_definition: The set of all vectors orthogonal to every vector in a given subspace.

concepts.name: Subspace
concepts.short_definition: A subset of a vector space that is itself a vector space under the same operations.

concepts.name: Projection
concepts.short_definition: The component of one vector that lies in the direction of another vector.

concepts.name: Orthogonal Projection
concepts.short_definition: A projection onto a subspace that is orthogonal to the remaining part of the vector.

concepts.name: Gram-Schmidt Process
concepts.short_definition: An algorithm for constructing an orthonormal basis from a set of linearly independent vectors.

concepts.name: Linear Independence
concepts.short_definition: A set of vectors where no vector can be written as a linear combination of the others.

concepts.name: Linear Combination
concepts.short_definition: A sum of scalar multiples of vectors.

concepts.name: Basis
concepts.short_definition: A set of linearly independent vectors that span a vector space.

concepts.name: Span
concepts.short_definition: The set of all possible linear combinations of a given set of vectors.

concepts.name: Orthonormal Basis
concepts.short_definition: A basis consisting of orthonormal vectors.

concepts.name: Coordinate Vector
concepts.short_definition: The representation of a vector in terms of a specific basis.

concepts.name: Adjoint Operator
concepts.short_definition: An operator related to a linear operator in an inner product space, generalizing the concept of transpose.

concepts.name: Linear Operator
concepts.short_definition: A function between vector spaces that preserves vector addition and scalar multiplication.

concepts.name: Self-Adjoint Operator
concepts.short_definition: A linear operator that is equal to its adjoint.

concepts.name: Unitary Operator
concepts.short_definition: A linear operator in a complex inner product space that preserves the inner product.

concepts.name: Orthogonal Operator
concepts.short_definition: A linear operator in a real inner product space that preserves the inner product.

concepts.name: Function Space
concepts.short_definition: A vector space where the elements are functions, often equipped with an inner product.

concepts.name: Integral
concepts.short_definition: A mathematical object that generalizes the notion of summation, often used to define inner products for functions.

concepts.name: Inner Product of Functions
concepts.short_definition: An inner product defined on a space of functions, typically involving integration.
